news.t: Disney shares surge nearly 10%, and some ETFs go along for the ride
Analysts Expect The IShares U.S. Consumer Services ETF Will Reach $250
It’s hard for shoppers to avoid Amazon — even in their ETFs
Charts Suggest Consumer Services Sector Is Headed Higher

holdings.infoBox: IYC is an equity ETF with a total of 171 individual holdings. The top holdings are Amazon.com stock at 12.88%, Tesla at 7.67%, Walmart at 4.18%, The Home Depot at 4.18%, and Costco Wholesale at 4.16%.

overview.description: The iShares U.S. Consumer Discretionary ETF (IYC) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in consumer discretionary equity. The fund tracks a market-cap-weighted index of US companies that provide consumer services. IYC was launched on Jun 12, 2000 and is issued by BlackRock.

data.title: ETF Spotlight: iShares US Consumer Discretionary ETF
data.text: Friday's ETF Spotlight highlights the consumer discretionary sector as Jefferies upgrades full-service restaurants to buy as small independent chains lose money.

data.title: Hyatt hotels jumps on luxury resort deal
data.text: CNBC's Seema Mody reports on the deal between Hyatt and Apple Leisure Group that will double Hyatt's resort portfolio.

data.title: Disney shares surge nearly 10%, and some ETFs go along for the ride
data.text: Exchange-traded funds with big holdings in Walt Disney Co. DIS, +9.91% jumped Wednesday, with some adding to gains that put them on track for their best week in over a month.

data.title: Analysts Expect The IShares U.S. Consumer Services ETF Will Reach $250
data.text: For the iShares U.S. Consumer Services ETF (IYC), we found that the implied analyst target price for the ETF based upon its underlying holdings is $250.12 per unit.

data.title: It’s hard for shoppers to avoid Amazon — even in their ETFs
data.text: Amazon.com Inc. makes up an outsize share of the biggest consumer discretionary ETFs, a fact investors should keep in mind when researching such a trade.

data.title: Charts Suggest Consumer Services Sector Is Headed Higher
data.text: Bullish chart patterns and nearby support levels make the consumer services sector one to watch heading into the final months of 2019.
